heading: Packsaddle vs Paddys Flat — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Packsaddle, New South Wales and Paddys Flat, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Packsaddle has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Paddys Flat (47/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Packsaddle or Paddys Flat safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Packsaddle has a flood risk score of 36/100, while Paddys Flat scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Packsaddle scores 22/100 for bushfire risk. Paddys Flat scores 59/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Packsaddle or Paddys Flat?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Packsaddle has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
